title: The nightly clipboard: one DAG, one sick task, one retry

steps.narration: 02:00. The scheduler fires nimbusmart_nightly for execution_date=2026-07-08. Nothing runs yet — the DAG's first task is a sensor, wait_for_orders_export, poking S3 every 60s for the OLTP team's _SUCCESS flag. Airflow is the shift supervisor reading the clipboard: nobody picks up a task before its upstream box is ticked.

state.note: sensor mode=reschedule: it frees its worker slot between pokes instead of blocking one for the whole wait

steps.narration: 02:07. Still poking. The orders export was due at 02:00 but the source OLTP snapshot is running long tonight. The sensor is not failing — it is waiting, exactly as designed. Its timeout is 45 minutes; we are 7 minutes in. Every downstream task stays in none/scheduled state, held behind this one tick.

state.note: downstream held: ingest, silver, fraud queue, gold — all blocked on one unticked box

steps.narration: 02:14. The _SUCCESS flag lands; the sensor clears on its next poke and marks itself success. Fourteen minutes of the night spent purely waiting — and that is the good outcome. The alternative, hard-coding a sleep(15m), would break the first night the export ran fast or slow.

state.note: sensor succeeded at 02:14 — the export was 14 min late; the DAG absorbed it without a page

steps.narration: Two ingest tasks fan out in parallel — they share no dependency, so the scheduler dispatches both the instant the sensor clears. ingest_orders_bronze lands 240 orders; ingest_fraud_scores_bronze lands 225 scores. This is the clipboard's real value: it knows which jobs can run at once and which must queue.

state.note: PythonOperator ×2, no edge between them → parallel; both write to the Bronze receiving dock

steps.narration: build_silver_orders depends on the orders ingest, so it starts only after that box is ticked — dependency order, not clock order. It standardizes types and drops the two malformed rows into quarantine. 6.5 minutes. Downstream, build_fraud_review_queue waits on both silver orders and the fraud ingest.

state.note: edge: ingest_orders_bronze → build_silver_orders; the QC station runs only after the dock signs off

steps.narration: 02:27. build_fraud_review_queue starts — the task that flags every order at or above FRAUD_REVIEW_THRESHOLD (0.80). Two minutes in, its executor is lost: a spot node reclaim kills the JVM mid-shuffle. The task fails. This is the supervisor's 'someone called in sick' moment.

state.note: ExecutorLostFailure at 02:29 — transient infra, not a code bug; the task record flips to up_for_retry

steps.narration: The DAG does not collapse. build_fraud_review_queue is configured with retries=2 and retry_delay=2m (exponential backoff). Airflow waits out the delay, then re-dispatches the same task instance with an incremented try number. Because the task is idempotent — it overwrites its output partition — a retry is safe to run from scratch.

state.note: held downstream: refresh_gold_dashboard stays scheduled until this task reaches success

steps.narration: Try 2 gets a healthy executor and completes cleanly in 4.8 minutes: 43 orders land in the review queue (4 of them exactly at 0.80). The task flips to success. One sick worker cost the DAG ~7 minutes of retry, not a failed night and not a 03:00 page.

state.note: review queue built: 43 orders ≥ FRAUD_REVIEW_THRESHOLD — same result the first try would have produced

steps.narration: With its last upstream box finally ticked, refresh_gold_dashboard releases and runs — 2.7 minutes to rebuild the executive mart. The whole DAG reaches success at 02:35. Total wall clock: 35 minutes, of which 14 was the sensor waiting and 7 was the retry — only 14 was actual compute.

state.note: DAG state = success · execution_date=2026-07-08 · all 6 tasks green

steps.narration: The clipboard's whole job, in one sentence: encode the dependencies once, and every night the DAG picks the right order, runs what it can in parallel, waits on what is not ready, and retries what falls over — without a human awake to sequence it. You describe the work as a graph; Airflow runs the shift.

state.note:
the DAG contract: sensors gate on upstream readiness · edges encode order · retries absorb transient failure · idempotent tasks make retries free
you author the graph; the scheduler owns the night
